child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: type
child.description: The type of the required data, specified as the type name of a resource. For profiles, this value is set to the type of the base...
child.longDesc: The type of the required data, specified as the type name of a resource. For profiles, this value is set to the type of the base resource of the profile.

child.name: profile
child.description: The profile of the required data, specified as the uri of the profile definition.

child.name: subjectCodeableConcept
child.description: The intended subjects of the data requirement. If this element is not provided, a Patient subject is assumed.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: subjectReference
child.description: The intended subjects of the data requirement. If this element is not provided, a Patient subject is assumed.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: mustSupport
child.description: Indicates that specific elements of the type are referenced by the knowledge module and must be supported by the consumer in ord...
child.longDesc:
Indicates that specific elements of the type are referenced by the knowledge module and must be supported by the consumer in order to obtain an effective evaluation. This does not mean that a value is required for this element, only that the consuming system must understand the element and be able to provide values for it if they are available.
The value of mustSupport SHALL be a FHIRPath resolvable on the type of the DataRequirement. The path SHALL consist only of identifiers, constant indexers, and .resolve() (see the [Simple FHIRPath Profile](fhirpath.html#simple) for full details).

child.name: codeFilter
child.description: Code filters specify additional constraints on the data, specifying the value set of interest for a particular element of the da...
child.longDesc: Code filters specify additional constraints on the data, specifying the value set of interest for a particular element of the data. Each code filter defines an additional constraint on the data, i.e. code filters are AND'ed, not OR'ed.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: path
child.description: The code-valued attribute of the filter. The specified path SHALL be a FHIRPath resolvable on the specified type of the DataRequ...
child.longDesc: The code-valued attribute of the filter. The specified path SHALL be a FHIRPath resolvable on the specified type of the DataRequirement, and SHALL consist only of identifiers, constant indexers, and .resolve(). The path is allowed to contain qualifiers (.) to traverse sub-elements, as well as indexers ([x]) to traverse multiple-cardinality sub-elements (see the [Simple FHIRPath Profile](fhirpath.html#simple) for full details). Note that the index must be an integer constant. The path must resolve to an element of type code, Coding, or CodeableConcept.

child.name: searchParam
child.description: A token parameter that refers to a search parameter defined on the specified type of the DataRequirement, and which searches on ...
child.longDesc: A token parameter that refers to a search parameter defined on the specified type of the DataRequirement, and which searches on elements of type code, Coding, or CodeableConcept.

child.name: valueSet
child.description: The valueset for the code filter. The valueSet and code elements are additive. If valueSet is specified, the filter will return ...
child.longDesc: The valueset for the code filter. The valueSet and code elements are additive. If valueSet is specified, the filter will return only those data items for which the value of the code-valued element specified in the path is a member of the specified valueset.

child.name: code
child.description: The codes for the code filter. If values are given, the filter will return only those data items for which the code-valued attri...
child.longDesc: The codes for the code filter. If values are given, the filter will return only those data items for which the code-valued attribute specified by the path has a value that is one of the specified codes. If codes are specified in addition to a value set, the filter returns items matching a code in the value set or one of the specified codes.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: dateFilter
child.description: Date filters specify additional constraints on the data in terms of the applicable date range for specific elements. Each date f...
child.longDesc: Date filters specify additional constraints on the data in terms of the applicable date range for specific elements. Each date filter specifies an additional constraint on the data, i.e. date filters are AND'ed, not OR'ed.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: path
child.description: The date-valued attribute of the filter. The specified path SHALL be a FHIRPath resolvable on the specified type of the DataRequ...
child.longDesc: The date-valued attribute of the filter. The specified path SHALL be a FHIRPath resolvable on the specified type of the DataRequirement, and SHALL consist only of identifiers, constant indexers, and .resolve(). The path is allowed to contain qualifiers (.) to traverse sub-elements, as well as indexers ([x]) to traverse multiple-cardinality sub-elements (see the [Simple FHIRPath Profile](fhirpath.html#simple) for full details). Note that the index must be an integer constant. The path must resolve to an element of type date, dateTime, Period, Schedule, or Timing.

child.name: searchParam
child.description: A date parameter that refers to a search parameter defined on the specified type of the DataRequirement, and which searches on e...
child.longDesc: A date parameter that refers to a search parameter defined on the specified type of the DataRequirement, and which searches on elements of type date, dateTime, Period, Schedule, or Timing.

child.name: valueDateTime
child.description: The value of the filter. If period is specified, the filter will return only those data items that fall within the bounds determ...
child.longDesc: The value of the filter. If period is specified, the filter will return only those data items that fall within the bounds determined by the Period, inclusive of the period boundaries. If dateTime is specified, the filter will return only those data items that are equal to the specified dateTime. If a Duration is specified, the filter will return only those data items that fall within Duration before now.

child.name: valuePeriod
child.description: The value of the filter. If period is specified, the filter will return only those data items that fall within the bounds determ...
child.longDesc: The value of the filter. If period is specified, the filter will return only those data items that fall within the bounds determined by the Period, inclusive of the period boundaries. If dateTime is specified, the filter will return only those data items that are equal to the specified dateTime. If a Duration is specified, the filter will return only those data items that fall within Duration before now.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: valueDuration
child.description: The value of the filter. If period is specified, the filter will return only those data items that fall within the bounds determ...
child.longDesc: The value of the filter. If period is specified, the filter will return only those data items that fall within the bounds determined by the Period, inclusive of the period boundaries. If dateTime is specified, the filter will return only those data items that are equal to the specified dateTime. If a Duration is specified, the filter will return only those data items that fall within Duration before now.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: valueFilter
child.description: Value filters specify additional constraints on the data for elements other than code-valued or date-valued. Each value filter s...
child.longDesc: Value filters specify additional constraints on the data for elements other than code-valued or date-valued. Each value filter specifies an additional constraint on the data (i.e. valueFilters are AND'ed, not OR'ed).

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: path
child.description: The attribute of the filter. The specified path SHALL be a FHIRPath resolvable on the specified type of the DataRequirement, and...
child.longDesc: The attribute of the filter. The specified path SHALL be a FHIRPath resolvable on the specified type of the DataRequirement, and SHALL consist only of identifiers, constant indexers, and .resolve(). The path is allowed to contain qualifiers (.) to traverse sub-elements, as well as indexers ([x]) to traverse multiple-cardinality sub-elements (see the [Simple FHIRPath Profile](fhirpath.html#simple) for full details). Note that the index must be an integer constant. The path must resolve to an element of a type that is comparable to the valueFilter.value[x] element for the filter.

child.name: searchParam
child.description: A search parameter defined on the specified type of the DataRequirement, and which searches on elements of a type compatible wit...
child.longDesc: A search parameter defined on the specified type of the DataRequirement, and which searches on elements of a type compatible with the type of the valueFilter.value[x] for the filter.

child.name: comparator
child.description: The comparator to be used to determine whether the value is matching.

child.name: valueDateTime
child.description: The value of the filter.

child.name: valuePeriod
child.description: The value of the filter.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: valueDuration
child.description: The value of the filter.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: limit
child.description: Specifies a maximum number of results that are required (uses the _count search parameter).

child.name: sort
child.description: Specifies the order of the results to be returned.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: path
child.description: The attribute of the sort. The specified path must be resolvable from the type of the required data. The path is allowed to cont...
child.longDesc: The attribute of the sort. The specified path must be resolvable from the type of the required data. The path is allowed to contain qualifiers (.) to traverse sub-elements, as well as indexers ([x]) to traverse multiple-cardinality sub-elements. Note that the index must be an integer constant.

child.name: direction
child.description: The direction of the sort, ascending or descending.
